explanation: This LTL property ensures that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode and the pilot is not in control (i.e., !standby), the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step. The chosen template captures this by directly specifying the desired state transition based on the current state and the pilot's control status.

The LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, and the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby), then in the next time step, the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state. This is achieved by checking if the autopilot is in STANDBY mode (bool_exp1) and the pilot is not in control (bool_exp2). If both conditions hold, then the property ensures that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state in the next time step (bool_exp3). The template used is a generalized form of the implication, which is suitable for expressing transitions between states based on certain conditions.

The LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot must transition from STANDBY to TRANSITION state when the pilot is not in control (not standby). It uses the G (globally) operator to ensure that this condition holds for all time steps. The boolean expression (! state_is_STANDBY) represents the condition where the pilot is not in control. The boolean expression (state_is_TRANSITION & ! state_is_STANDBY) represents the requirement that the autopilot must be in the TRANSITION state in the next time step. The overall property ensures that whenever the pilot is not in control, the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state in the next time step.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, the next state should be TRANSITION if the pilot is not in control (not standby). It uses a temporal implication (G (bool_exp2 -> X bool_exp3)) to express this condition. The boolean expressions bool_exp2 and bool_exp3 are replaced with the atomic propositions representing STANDBY and TRANSITION states, respectively.

This is captured by the chosen template, which checks if the autopilot is in the STANDBY state and the pilot is not in control, and then asserts that in the next state, the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state. The chosen template ID is 1, and the boolean expression is "(!state_is_STANDBY) & state_is_STANDBY & state_is_TRANSITION".

The boolean expression bool_exp3 is (state_is_TRANSITION) which is true when the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state.  The chosen LTL template is G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)) which enforces that whenever bool_exp2 is true, bool_exp3 will be true in the next time step.  This is the classic LTL encoding for "whenever A, then next B".
